description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the alpine:latest Docker image and defines three stages: build, package, and deploy. In the build stage add a compile-test job that runs echo "Build $CI_COMMIT_SHA in branch $CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG with tag $CI_COMMIT_TAG" and a static-analysis job that runs echo "Static Code Analysis, parallel run with compiling". In the package stage add a build-docker-image job that runs echo "Build $CI_COMMIT_SHA in branch $CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG with tag $CI_COMMIT_TAG", then cat README.md > README-$CI_COMMIT_SHA.md, then echo "Version $CI_COMMIT_SHA" >> README-$CI_COMMIT_SHA.md, and publishes README-$CI_COMMIT_SHA.md as an artifact. In the deploy stage add three jobs: deploy-to-development runs only for refs matching the regex /-M\./, echoes "Deploy build" +$CI_COMMIT_SHA+ " to development server" and cats the generated README file; deploy-to-testing runs only for refs matching /-RC\./, echoes "Deploy build" +$CI_COMMIT_SHA+ " to testing server" and cats the README; deploy-to-production runs only for refs matching /-RELEASE$/, is manual, echoes "Deploy build" +$CI_COMMIT_SHA+ " to production server" and cats the README.
